currentUse: Protestant church — the former St. Casimir church and school buildings at 1368 West 15th Avenue are used by the Black-majority Power and Light Church of Gary, an independent congregation led by Apostle Benny L. Dozier Sr. and Prophetess Geraldine Dozier
summary: The 1927 St. Casimir Lithuanian church building in Gary still stands and remains in religious use: per the Global True Lithuania heritage survey, both the church and the adjacent former school building (built 1918 as the first church) at 1368 West 15th Avenue are now used "for the same purposes" by the Black-majority Power and Light church. The Power & Light Church of Gary is an active congregation as of mid-2026, with its own website listing an 11 AM Sunday service and leadership by Apostle Benny L. Dozier Sr. and Prophetess Geraldine Dozier; its official address is given as 1351 Lincoln St, Gary, IN (zip 46407, the same zip as the old St. Casimir parish listing). Global True Lithuania records that after the sale the newer decorative elements — post-1970-fire stained glass by designer A. Valeška and Lithuanian folk-inspired wooden artwork — were removed, but that two original Lithuanian stained-glass windows inscribed "Moterų dovana" and "Vyrų dovana" survived the April 12, 1970 fire and remained in the building. The same source confirms the closure trajectory in the archive record: after most Lithuanians left Gary and the 1970 fire, parishioners sought to relocate the parish to the suburbs and had found a buyer for the church, but the diocese forbade the relocation, effectively condemning the parish, which closed in 1998. A 2016 Hoosier History Live newsletter photo caption gives the closure as 2000 — a minor discrepancy among secondary sources. No sale price, buyer transaction details, recent press, preservation campaign, or reunion activity was found.

developments.headline: Parish closed after diocese blocked suburban relocation; buildings sold to Power and Light church
developments.detail: Global True Lithuania's Indiana heritage survey records that St. Casimir (church built 1927, school/first church built 1918) closed in 1998 after the diocese forbade parishioners' plan to relocate to the suburbs even though they had found a buyer for the church. After the sale, the post-1970-fire stained glass by A. Valeška and Lithuanian folk-style wooden artwork were removed; two original Lithuanian stained-glass windows ('Moterų dovana' and 'Vyrų dovana'), the only ones to survive the April 12, 1970 fire, remained. Both buildings at 1368 West 15th Avenue passed to the Black-majority Power and Light church, which uses them for worship.

developments.headline: Power & Light Church of Gary is an active congregation as of 2026
developments.detail: The Power & Light Church of Gary maintains a live website listing an 11 AM Sunday service, leadership by Apostle Benny L. Dozier Sr. and Prophetess Geraldine Dozier, and an official address of 1351 Lincoln St, Gary, IN — a location in zip 46407, the same zip as the former St. Casimir parish. The site itself does not mention the building's Catholic or Lithuanian past, so continued occupancy of the 15th Avenue buildings specifically rests on the Global True Lithuania survey.

sources.title: Our Church – Power & Light Church of Gary
Our Church – Power & Light Church of Gary
Latvian and Lithuanian heritage (newsletter, photo caption on Gary St. Casimir)

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions; two refinements and one secondary-source discrepancy. (1) The archive's "rescue committee concluded saving the parish was impossible; bishop closed it unilaterally" is refined by Global True Lithuania: parishioners actively tried to RELOCATE the parish to the suburbs and had found a buyer for the church, but the diocese forbade the relocation, condemning the parish — consistent with, and more specific than, the archive framing. (2) Closure year: Global True Lithuania and an nwitimes.com search snippet agree with the archive's 1998, but a 2016 Hoosier History Live photo caption says the church "closed in 2000" — treated as an error or a reference to a later final event, since the weight of sources supports 1998. (3) Archive has no founding year; sources give the first church/school building as 1918 and the main church as 1927 (construction dates, not necessarily parish founding date).
gaps: Sale price, exact sale year, and the legal buyer entity were not published in any source found (Power and Light church is named as the occupant/purchaser context by Global True Lithuania, but no transaction record was located). The Power & Light Church's own website gives its address as 1351 Lincoln St rather than 1368 W 15th Ave — plausibly the same corner property, but I could not independently confirm the two addresses refer to the same parcel, nor verify via county records that the congregation still occupies the former St. Casimir buildings in 2026 (the Global True Lithuania page is undated). A relevant nwitimes.com article ("Lithuanian heritage on parade," confirming the 1998 closure and that Lithuanian celebrations were held at St. Casimir until then) is paywalled and could not be opened. No recent press (2020s), diocesan statements, preservation efforts, or reunion coverage for this parish was found in English or Lithuanian searches. Parish founding year remains unverified. Physical condition of the buildings today (beyond "in use") is unknown.
